question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R4 (PE2) changes its SRv6 locator PE2 prefix from `fcbb:bb00:4::/48` to
`fcbb:bb00:2::/48` — the same prefix already used by R2's PE1 locator.
Two SRv6 locators advertise overlapping /48 prefixes. IS-IS LSDB sees
the same prefix from two sources → routes alternate or one wins via
tiebreak. SRv6 End/End.X SIDs encoded in fcbb:bb00:2::xxxx now have
ambiguous resolution — packets destined to PE2's SRv6 functions may
be forwarded to PE1's locator.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.104 (R4, PE2), the SRv6 locator PE2 prefix was changed from fcbb:bb00:4::/48 to fcbb:bb00:2::/48 - the same prefix already used by R2's PE1 locator.
Two locators now advertise the overlapping /48, which violates locator-prefix uniqueness across PEs, so IS-IS cannot unambiguously bind the prefix to a single owner and the SRv6 End/End.X SIDs encoded under fcbb:bb00:2:: are no longer resolvable to PE2 alone; packets for PE2's SRv6 functions may be forwarded to PE1 instead.
Fix: restore R4's PE2 locator prefix to fcbb:bb00:4::/48.
expected_symptoms_summary:
No fault syslog observed after apply — silent scenario; SRv6
locator prefix changes are quiet config events. The duplicate-prefix
conflict is visible via CLI probe. Diagnostic ground truth lives in
expected_state_diffs.
expected_state_diffs: R4 `show segment-routing srv6 locator`: PE2 advertises prefix fcbb:bb00:2::/48 (baseline: fcbb:bb00:4::/48). The per-PE assignment PE1=::2, TRANSIT=::3, PE2=::4 is violated.
Any router `show segment-routing srv6 locator`: TWO locators advertise fcbb:bb00:2::/48 — R2's PE1 (Owner=local for R2) and R4's PE2 (Owner=local for R4).
R3 `show route ipv6 fcbb:bb00:2::/48`: two IS-IS sources (R2 and R4); best-path tiebreak picks one and End/End.X SIDs encoded under fcbb:bb00:2:: may forward to the wrong PE.
R4 `show running-config segment-routing srv6 locators | section PE2`: prefix line shows fcbb:bb00:2::/48 — diff vs R4's baseline (fcbb:bb00:4::/48).

apply.commands:
configure terminal
segment-routing
 srv6
  locators
   locator PE2
    prefix fcbb:bb00:2::/48

revert.commands:
configure terminal
segment-routing
 srv6
  locators
   locator PE2
    prefix fcbb:bb00:4::/48
